statement.p: Find the slope-intercept form of an equation for the line that passes through the points (-3,150) and (0,30)\text{.}

solution.p: We first find the slope between our two points: (\overset{x_1}{-3},\overset{y_1}{150}) and (\overset{x_2}{0},\overset{y_2}{30})\text{.} Using the slope formula, we have:
Now we can write y=-40x+b and to find b we need look no further than one of the given points: (0,30)\text{.} The value of b is 30\text{.} So, the slope-intercept form of the line is y=-40x+30\text{.}
